title: Week Ahead — Market Outlook
headline: Markets face a catalyst-heavy week where inflation prints, Fed Chair testimony, and the start of earnings season could reprice rate expectations quickly.
volatilityRationale: With CPI/PPI, Fed Chair Warsh testimony, and major bank/mega-cap-adjacent earnings clustered in a single week, small surprises can cascade across rates, equities, and the dollar even if realized volatility remains contained.

recap.text: FOMC minutes from the June 16–17 meeting were released, keeping policy-path uncertainty in focus ahead of the late-July FOMC.
CNBC previewed the coming week’s setup (inflation data plus earnings season) while noting geopolitical risk as an overhang for sentiment.
The Fed’s July calendar highlighted the near-term path to the July 28–29 FOMC meeting and related communications (including Warsh testimony and Beige Book timing).

triggers.text: US Retail Sales (June) + weekly jobless claims
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh semiannual monetary policy testimony (House then Senate)
Start of major US bank earnings (JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C) and other key reports (BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, ASML, TSM, Netflix)

watch.text: Volatility regime: VIX is in the mid-teens, implying markets are not pricing a large near-term shock despite a dense event calendar.
Narrative risk: headline-driven geopolitical developments can move energy and rates quickly, amplifying CPI reactions.
Rates sensitivity: testimony and inflation data may shift expectations for the July 28–29 FOMC meeting and beyond.

sources.title: TradingCharts — US economic calendar (week of July 13, 2026)
